macro_summary: U.S. equities edged higher Monday as chip stocks rebounded from last week's steep selloff, with the S&P 500 adding 0.74% and the Nasdaq gaining 1.21%. SOXX surged 2.15%, led by AMD and TSMC, while oil steadied at $68.82—fully unwinding the Iran war premium. Fed Governor Waller pushed back against recent dovish bets, calling high inflation the primary risk and keeping a July rate hike on the table, even as the 10-year yield slipped to 4.48%.
commodities_note: WTI at $68.82 is back to late-February levels, down 28% over the past month, as OPEC+ raised output targets and Hormuz shipping resumes. Gold dipped 0.44% to $4,158.50. VIX fell 1.52% to 15.57—a multi-month low that signals considerable complacency heading into CPI next week.

thesis_highlights: NVDA fiscal year offset from CY: Q4 FY2026 print = $68.1B (+73%), Q1 FY2027 guide = $78B (Feb 26 2026 earnings call). Vera Rubin samples shipping but contribute no booked revenue — Blackwell + Hopper still drive the print.

writeup.headline: Blackwell $78B guide intact, but tight stop distorts R:R — sizing R:R 3.9:1 to structural support.
writeup.what_to_do: The Q1 FY2027 guide of $78B implies Blackwell is shipping ahead of schedule. Headline R:R is distorted by a 0.4% stop; sizing R:R to the 200-day SMA at $191.12 is a more honest 3.9:1. Upgrade to ACCUMULATE requires a reclaim of $197.62 resistance on above-average volume and an expansion of the tight invalidation. Fragility: single catalyst (Blackwell ramp) — multi-leg thesis required for ACCUMULATE.
writeup.tight_upside_disclosure: Nearest resistance at $197.62 (1.1% above) — headline R:R only materializes once that level breaks on above-average volume.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if NVDA fails to reclaim SMA50 ($209.66) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

singapore.summary: All three banks remain CAUTION as extension surges above 50-day averages (DBS +8%, OCBC +9.2%, UOB +7.1%). NIM compression from US rates above 4.20% and MAS S$NEER steepness remains the structural headwind. UOB’s 11.2x forward PE stands out as a deep discount, but RSI of 74.5 signals exhaustion.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — NIM headwinds persist, technical extension blocks entry.
singapore.key_development: UOB trades at a 22% discount to cluster median PE, the cheapest of the trio, but RSI 74.5 is overbought and blocks fresh capital.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is diverging: NVDA holds WATCH while most names are hard-blocked on extension (AMD +18.6%, MU +14.2%, ASML +10.3%) or trapped in failed catalysts (INTC RCP gap-fill, TSEM). SOXX rebounded 2.15% Monday, but the -1.4% weekly decline shows the AI capex debate is far from resolved. Sk Hynix’s $28B Nasdaq listing pricing pressure added volatility.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically blocked — multiple names extended above 50-day averages.
semiconductors.key_development: Goldman Sachs dropped TSM from its Asia-Pacific Conviction List (TheStreet, July 6), a notable warning on near-term valuation, while Bernstein hiked ASML’s target.

big_tech.summary: BigTech signals have stabilized: MSFT upgraded to HOLD as the extension block cleared, though R:R remains distorted. GOOG/AMZN hold steady, with Google’s TPU ramp and Meta lease talks providing positive offsets. PLTR’s 5-day +17% momentum chase keeps it blocked. The cluster lacks actionable entries ahead of earnings; the Jul 23 (GOOG) and Jul 29 (MSFT) prints are the next catalysts.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautious — AI capex sustainability debated, but cloud fundamentals steady.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT’s Xbox restructuring is noise; the Azure non-OpenAI RPO trajectory at the Jul 29 print is the clean read.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Marvell announces a second hyperscaler 5nm custom ASIC win with volume commitment.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Arista’s Q2 print shows 800G port shipments accelerating above 200K units and guides above consensus.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz shipping recovers, but US-Iran de-escalation terms remain unsettled; oil risk premium fully evaporated.
Fed Governor Waller warns inflation risks are to the upside, challenging the dovish payrolls narrative and keeping a July rate hike on the table.
US-China tech decoupling continues, but agricultural cooperation signals a tactical thaw; no new blacklist additions this week.
ECB cuts rates to 2022 low, diverging from a potentially hawkish Fed; DXY flat at 100.89, but euro weakness could accelerate.
geopolitical.market_impact: The collapse in oil prices back to pre-Iran war levels has removed a key inflation driver, but Waller’s hawkish pushback keeps rate hike fears alive, capping tech multiples. Semis benefit from lower energy costs, but the VIX at 15.57 signals dangerous complacency ahead of CPI. AI demand remains the structural anchor, but entry points are scarce.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Waller explicitly tilted risks toward high inflation in Rome speech, stating the labor market has stabilized and hiking is on the table.

base.description: Fed holds rates through summer; oil steady near $70; AI capex continues but at a measured pace. Semis grind sideways between 50-day and 200-day averages; Big Tech earnings deliver in-line results. Select entry on pullbacks to SMA50s.

optimistic.description: June CPI comes in below 4.0%, dousing rate hike bets; AI demand accelerates (NVDA $78B guide confirmed). Semis break above overhead resistance; Singapore banks rally on yield curve steepening. Position for a Q3 rally — add NVDA, ASML on dips.

pessimistic.description: CPI above 4.5%, Fed hikes in July; US10Y spikes above 4.8%. Semis sell off 5-7% as valuations reprice; extended names (AMD, MU) lead declines. Defense names (PLTR) hold bid. Raise cash, trim extended positions.

wildcard.description: A sudden US-Iran breakthrough triggers a geopolitical risk-off unwind, collapsing oil below $60. Energy deflation crashes inflation expectations, forcing a Fed pause. Semis explode higher on margin relief. Buy NVDA, TSM aggressively.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold current positions; no new entry until CPI resolves rate uncertainty. Protect against complacency with VIX at 15.57; consider hedging with VIX calls.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautious — limited entry opportunities amidst extension blocks and binary risks.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 8 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; semis dominate, but AI demand narrative intact. VIX at 15.57 signals complacency — protect gains.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=3) / ACCUMULATE (n=5) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
